items.title: Perform ICMP ping sweep across in-scope ranges
items.description: Send ICMP echo, timestamp, and address-mask requests across the target CIDR ranges to map responsive hosts before port scanning. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Host Discovery: Confirm which hosts respond to ICMP and which require alternate discovery; document filtered ranges where ICMP is dropped
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for perform icmp ping sweep across in-scope ranges, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enumerate local hosts via ARP scanning
items.description: On a local segment, ARP requests reliably enumerate live hosts even when ICMP is filtered, since ARP cannot be firewalled at layer 2. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Host Discovery: Verify the full list of MAC/IP pairs on the local broadcast domain and cross-reference against expected asset inventory
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate local hosts via arp scanning, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Discover hosts behind ICMP filtering via TCP/UDP probes
items.description: Use TCP SYN/ACK and UDP probes to common ports to identify hosts that drop ICMP but still respond to transport-layer probes. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Host Discovery: Confirm hosts found via TCP/UDP that were missed by ICMP; ensure no in-scope subnet is silently skipped
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for discover hosts behind icmp filtering via tcp/udp probes, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Validate discovered hosts against the agreed scope
items.description: Cross-check every discovered live host against the engagement scope to avoid testing out-of-scope or third-party systems. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Host Discovery: Cross-check every discovered live host against the engagement scope to avoid testing out-of-scope or third-party systems
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for validate discovered hosts against the agreed scope, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Run a full TCP port scan (all 65535 ports)
items.description: A full-range TCP scan ensures services running on non-standard high ports are not missed by a default top-1000 scan. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Port Scanning: Confirm all 65535 TCP ports were scanned per host and that any open high ports are followed up with service detection
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for run a full tcp port scan (all 65535 ports), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Scan key UDP services
items.description: UDP services (DNS, SNMP, NetBIOS, TFTP, IKE, NTP) are frequently overlooked yet expose high-value attack surface and amplification vectors. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Port Scanning: Verify that common UDP ports are scanned and that open|filtered results are validated with service-specific probes
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for scan key udp services, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Tune scan timing and apply IDS/firewall evasion
items.description: Aggressive scanning can trip IDS/IPS and corrupt results; tune timing templates, fragmentation, and decoys to balance stealth and accuracy. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Port Scanning: Confirm scan timing matches rules of engagement; verify whether evasion (fragmentation, decoys, source-port spoofing) changes results, indicating filtering
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for tune scan timing and apply ids/firewall evasion, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Use a fast mass scanner for large ranges then confirm with nmap
items.description: For large external ranges, a fast async scanner identifies open ports quickly; results should be re-confirmed with nmap service detection to reduce false positives. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — Port Scanning: Verify mass-scan open ports are re-validated with a slower accurate scan and that rate limits did not drop responses
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for use a fast mass scanner for large ranges then confirm with nmap, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Host Discovery & Scanning — OS & Service Fingerprinting

items.title: Perform OS fingerprinting on live hosts
items.description: TCP/IP stack fingerprinting identifies the operating system and version, which narrows down applicable exploits and EOL risk. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — OS & Service Fingerprinting: Confirm OS guesses with multiple data points; flag end-of-life operating systems (e.g
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for perform os fingerprinting on live hosts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect service and version banners on open ports
items.description: Service/version detection maps each open port to a product and version, the foundation for CVE matching and default-credential checks. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — OS & Service Fingerprinting: Verify every open port has an identified service/version and capture banners for offline CVE lookup
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect service and version banners on open ports, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Run safe NSE discovery scripts for deeper fingerprinting
items.description: Nmap Scripting Engine default/discovery scripts extract richer service metadata (TLS info, SMB OS, HTTP titles) without intrusive exploitation. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — OS & Service Fingerprinting: Confirm default/discovery NSE categories were run and review output for misconfigurations and exposed metadata
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for run safe nse discovery scripts for deeper fingerprinting, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Flag end-of-life operating systems and services
items.description: EOL software no longer receives security patches and represents persistent critical risk regardless of current known CVEs. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Host Discovery & Scanning — OS & Service Fingerprinting: Verify identified OS/service versions against vendor EOL dates and document unsupported systems as findings
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for flag end-of-life operating systems and services, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test for SMB/RPC null and guest sessions
items.description: Null or guest sessions can leak users, groups, shares, and password policy without credentials, fueling further attacks. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SMB / NetBIOS: Verify whether anonymous/guest binds succeed and what information (users, shares, policies) is exposed without authentication
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for smb/rpc null and guest sessions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enumerate SMB shares and access permissions
items.description: Readable/writable shares often expose sensitive files, scripts, credentials, and backups accessible to low-privileged or anonymous users. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SMB / NetBIOS: Verify which shares are listable and read/write accessible, and spider readable shares for secrets and credentials
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate smb shares and access permissions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check SMB signing requirement
items.description: Hosts with SMB signing not required are vulnerable to NTLM relay attacks, enabling authentication coercion to be relayed for code execution. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SMB / NetBIOS: Verify signing:False hosts and confirm they are viable relay targets in combination with poisoning/coercion
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check smb signing requirement, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test for MS17-010 (EternalBlue) and legacy SMBv1
items.description: MS17-010 enables unauthenticated remote code execution over SMBv1 and remains common on unpatched/EOL Windows hosts. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SMB / NetBIOS: Verify SMBv1 is enabled and whether the MS17-010 check reports the host as vulnerable; confirm safely before any exploitation
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for ms17-010 (eternalblue) and legacy smbv1, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test for default/weak SNMP community strings
items.description: Default community strings such as public/private grant read (or read-write) access to extensive device configuration data. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SNMP: Verify whether common community strings are valid and whether read-write access is possible (enabling config changes)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for default/weak snmp community strings, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Perform SNMP walk to extract system information
items.description: SNMP walks can leak running processes, installed software, network interfaces, ARP tables, routes, and even local user accounts. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SNMP: Verify what sensitive data is reachable via SNMP walk (e.g
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for perform snmp walk to extract system information, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify SNMPv1/v2c cleartext exposure
items.description: SNMPv1 and v2c send community strings and data in cleartext, allowing interception; only SNMPv3 provides authentication and encryption. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SNMP: Verify the SNMP version in use and whether v3 with authPriv is enforced; flag cleartext v1/v2c on sensitive devices
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify snmpv1/v2c cleartext exposure, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Assess SNMP read-write access impact
items.description: A read-write community string can allow reconfiguring network devices, exfiltrating configs (e.g. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — SNMP: Verify read-write capability and document the highest-impact action possible (config export, interface changes) without disrupting production
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for assess snmp read-write access impact, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK - Network Device CLI / SNMP

items.title: Test for anonymous FTP access
items.description: Anonymous FTP can expose downloadable files and, when writable, allow upload of webshells or tampering with served content. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — FTP & Telnet: Verify anonymous login works, whether the root is readable/writable, and what data is exposed
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for anonymous ftp access, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify cleartext FTP/Telnet authentication
items.description: FTP and Telnet transmit credentials in cleartext, allowing interception on shared media or via MITM positioning. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — FTP & Telnet: Verify whether FTPS/SFTP/SSH replacements are enforced; capture a sample exchange to confirm cleartext credentials in transit
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify cleartext ftp/telnet authentication, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test FTP/Telnet for weak credentials
items.description: Legacy services often retain weak or default service accounts that allow authenticated access and data access or pivoting. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — FTP & Telnet: Verify credential strength via controlled, rate-limited password guessing aligned with lockout policy and rules of engagement
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test ftp/telnet for weak credentials, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check FTP/Telnet daemons for known CVEs
items.description: Specific FTP daemons (vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor, ProFTPD mod_copy) and old telnetd builds have known RCE vulnerabilities. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — FTP & Telnet: Verify daemon version against known vulnerable builds and confirm exploitability safely before any active testing
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check ftp/telnet daemons for known cves, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Attempt DNS zone transfer (AXFR)
items.description: A misconfigured DNS server allowing AXFR discloses the full zone, mapping internal hostnames, IPs, and infrastructure. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — DNS: Verify whether AXFR succeeds from an unauthorized host and document the leaked records
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for attempt dns zone transfer (axfr), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enumerate DNS records and subdomains
items.description: Brute-forcing records and subdomains expands the known attack surface and reveals dev/staging and internal-only hosts. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — DNS: Verify the set of resolvable hosts/subdomains and identify any non-production or forgotten systems in scope
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate dns records and subdomains, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check for open recursive resolver
items.description: Open resolvers can be abused for DNS amplification DDoS and cache poisoning, harming the organization and third parties. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — DNS: Verify whether the server recurses for arbitrary clients and estimate amplification factor for ANY/large responses
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check for open recursive resolver, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Query DNS server version and software
items.description: version.bind queries can disclose the resolver software/version, aiding CVE matching against known BIND/Windows DNS issues. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — DNS: Verify whether version.bind is exposed and map disclosed versions to known vulnerabilities
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for query dns server version and software, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify RDP/SSH exposed to untrusted networks
items.description: Internet-facing RDP/SSH is a primary initial-access vector; exposure should be flagged even before credential testing. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — RDP & SSH: Verify which remote-access services are reachable from untrusted networks and whether MFA/jump-host controls are absent
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify rdp/ssh exposed to untrusted networks, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test RDP/SSH for weak or reused credentials
items.description: Weak, default, or reused credentials on RDP/SSH frequently provide direct interactive access to hosts. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — RDP & SSH: Verify credential strength via controlled guessing within lockout policy; confirm whether key-based auth and MFA are enforced for SSH
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test rdp/ssh for weak or reused credentials, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Review SSH configuration weaknesses
items.description: Permissive SSH config (root login, password auth, weak KEX/ciphers, agent forwarding) increases attack surface and lateral-movement risk. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — RDP & SSH: Verify whether root login and password auth are allowed and whether weak key-exchange/ciphers are negotiable
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for review ssh configuration weaknesses, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check RDP for BlueKeep (CVE-2019-0708) and missing NLA
items.description: BlueKeep is a wormable pre-auth RDP RCE; missing Network Level Authentication also weakens RDP against credential and DoS attacks. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — RDP & SSH: Verify whether NLA is enforced and whether the host is reported vulnerable to BlueKeep; confirm safely before exploitation
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check rdp for bluekeep (cve-2019-0708) and missing nla, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test for unauthenticated MongoDB/Redis access
items.description: MongoDB and Redis frequently ship bound to all interfaces without authentication, exposing full data read/write to anyone on the network. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — Exposed Databases: Verify whether the service allows unauthenticated connection and what data/commands are accessible (including RCE primitives in Redis)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for unauthenticated mongodb/redis access, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test MSSQL for weak sa credentials and xp_cmdshell
items.description: A weak sa password on MSSQL can lead to command execution via xp_cmdshell and lateral movement via linked servers. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — Exposed Databases: Verify sa/login strength and whether xp_cmdshell or linked-server abuse is possible from the obtained access
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test mssql for weak sa credentials and xp_cmdshell, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test MySQL for weak credentials and over-privilege
items.description: Weak MySQL credentials plus FILE privilege or remote root can expose data and, in some cases, lead to file read/write or code execution. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — Exposed Databases: Verify credential strength, whether root is reachable remotely, and whether dangerous privileges (FILE) are granted
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test mysql for weak credentials and over-privilege, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify databases exposed beyond their app tier
items.description: Databases should only be reachable from their application tier; broader network exposure increases breach and ransomware impact. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Service Enumeration — Exposed Databases: Verify network reachability of each database port from untrusted segments and document segmentation gaps
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify databases exposed beyond their app tier, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Run unauthenticated vulnerability scans
items.description: Unauthenticated scanning models an external attacker view, surfacing exposed and exploitable network services. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Vulnerability Scanning: Verify scan coverage of all live hosts/ports and that scan policies match the engagement (safe checks vs
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for run unauthenticated vulnerability scans, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Run authenticated/credentialed scans
items.description: Credentialed scans inspect installed patches and configuration from the inside, finding issues invisible to network-only scans. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Vulnerability Scanning: Verify credentials authenticate successfully on each host and that the scan reports patch levels and local misconfigurations
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for run authenticated/credentialed scans, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Triage and validate scanner findings
items.description: Automated scanners produce false positives and inflated severities; manual validation is required before reporting. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Vulnerability Scanning: Verify each high/critical finding manually and confirm exploitability or downgrade/remove false positives
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for triage and validate scanner findings, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Run targeted template-based checks for known issues
items.description: Template-driven scanners quickly confirm specific known-vulnerable conditions (exposed panels, misconfigs, CVEs) with high precision. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Vulnerability Scanning: Verify template matches against actual service responses and confirm there is no false match before logging a finding
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for run targeted template-based checks for known issues, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Vulnerability Identification — Missing Patches & EOL Software

items.title: Map service versions to known CVEs
items.description: Version-to-CVE mapping highlights exploitable services; prioritize those in active-exploitation catalogs. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Missing Patches & EOL Software: Verify each version-matched CVE applies to the actual configuration and check whether it is in the CISA KEV catalog
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for map service versions to known cves, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify unsupported/EOL services and OSes
items.description: EOL components receive no patches and constitute standing risk; they should be reported regardless of current CVE count. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Missing Patches & EOL Software: Verify identified versions against vendor EOL schedules and quantify exposure of unsupported systems
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify unsupported/eol services and oses, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Prioritize unauthenticated RCE-class vulnerabilities
items.description: Pre-auth remote code execution on exposed services represents the most severe and time-sensitive findings. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Missing Patches & EOL Software: Verify presence of known pre-auth RCE conditions and confirm safely; escalate critical findings immediately per rules of engagement
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for prioritize unauthenticated rce-class vulnerabilities, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect missing hardening and patch baseline drift
items.description: Hosts drifting from a hardening baseline accumulate missing patches and weakened settings over time. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Missing Patches & EOL Software: Verify patch levels and security settings against an agreed baseline and document deviations
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect missing hardening and patch baseline drift, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test network devices for vendor default credentials
items.description: Routers, switches, printers, IPMI/iDRAC, and appliances frequently retain documented default credentials. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Default & Weak Credentials: Verify whether documented vendor defaults grant access to management interfaces and what control that access provides
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test network devices for vendor default credentials, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test services for weak/blank passwords
items.description: Weak or blank passwords on exposed services (SMB, SSH, RDP, DB, web panels) provide easy authenticated footholds. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Default & Weak Credentials: Verify password strength via controlled guessing within lockout policy and document any successful weak-credential access
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test services for weak/blank passwords, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test exposed management panels for default logins
items.description: Tomcat Manager, Jenkins, web admin consoles, and similar panels often ship with default or guessable credentials enabling RCE. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Default & Weak Credentials: Verify default credentials against discovered panels and assess the post-auth impact (deploy/exec) if access succeeds
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test exposed management panels for default logins, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Confirm lockout and rate-limiting before spraying
items.description: Password attacks risk account lockout and outages; verifying lockout policy first prevents disruption and aligns with rules of engagement. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Vulnerability Identification — Default & Weak Credentials: Verify lockout thresholds/observation windows and tune spraying volume/timing to stay within safe limits
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for confirm lockout and rate-limiting before spraying, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Validate exploits in a safe manner before use
items.description: Confirm an exploit applies and is non-destructive (or coordinate a maintenance window) before running it against production services. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Remote Service Exploitation: Verify exploit/target version match, assess crash risk, and obtain authorization for any potentially disruptive action
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for validate exploits in a safe manner before use, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Demonstrate remote code execution on vulnerable services
items.description: Proving RCE (e.g. Validate this during security reviews, under Exploitation & Pivoting — Remote Service Exploitation. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Remote Service Exploitation: Verify a controlled command-execution proof (e.g
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for demonstrate remote code execution on vulnerable services, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK - Exploitation of Remote Services (T1210)

items.title: Attempt local privilege escalation post-foothold
items.description: Initial access is often low-privileged; escalating to root/SYSTEM enables credential harvesting and broader impact. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Remote Service Exploitation: Verify achievable privilege level after foothold and document the escalation path (kernel, service, misconfig)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for attempt local privilege escalation post-foothold, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Track artifacts and clean up after exploitation
items.description: Payloads, accounts, and uploaded files must be logged and removed to avoid leaving the environment less secure. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Remote Service Exploitation: Verify a record of every artifact created and confirm removal at engagement end; report anything that cannot be safely removed
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for track artifacts and clean up after exploitation, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Establish port forwarding through a compromised host
items.description: Local/remote/dynamic port forwarding exposes internal services to the tester through a foothold host. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Pivoting & Tunneling: Verify forwarded access to internal-only services and document the reachable internal attack surface
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for establish port forwarding through a compromised host, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Pivot through a SOCKS proxy with proxychains
items.description: A SOCKS tunnel plus proxychains lets standard tools reach internal segments through the foothold host. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Pivoting & Tunneling: Verify tooling routes correctly through the proxy and confirm reachability of internal hosts that were previously unreachable
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for pivot through a socks proxy with proxychains, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Map and test network segmentation boundaries
items.description: Pivoting reveals whether segmentation actually isolates sensitive zones (PCI, OT, management) from general user networks. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Pivoting & Tunneling: Verify which protected segments are reachable from a foothold and document segmentation control failures
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for map and test network segmentation boundaries, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Chain multiple pivots to reach deep segments
items.description: Multi-hop pivoting reaches segments isolated from the initial foothold, demonstrating defense-in-depth gaps. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Pivoting & Tunneling: Verify a stable multi-hop tunnel and confirm reachability of deep/restricted segments through the chain
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for chain multiple pivots to reach deep segments, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Exploitation & Pivoting — Lateral Movement & Credential Reuse

items.title: Test cleartext credential reuse across hosts
items.description: Reused local-admin or service credentials allow rapid spread across many hosts once one is compromised. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Lateral Movement & Credential Reuse: Verify how many hosts accept the same credentials and which grant administrative access
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test cleartext credential reuse across hosts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test pass-the-hash with harvested NTLM hashes
items.description: NTLM hashes can authenticate without cracking, enabling lateral movement to hosts sharing the same local admin hash. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Lateral Movement & Credential Reuse: Verify which hosts accept the harvested hash and whether reuse of a single local-admin hash enables wide movement
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test pass-the-hash with harvested ntlm hashes, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Demonstrate remote execution via SMB/WMI/WinRM
items.description: Valid credentials enable remote command execution through native protocols, the backbone of practical lateral movement. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Lateral Movement & Credential Reuse: Verify which remote-exec methods succeed with obtained credentials and capture controlled proof of execution
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for demonstrate remote execution via smb/wmi/winrm, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Harvest additional credentials from compromised hosts
items.description: Memory, registry, files, and config often hold further credentials that unlock new hosts and accounts. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exploitation & Pivoting — Lateral Movement & Credential Reuse: Verify what credentials/secrets are recoverable from compromised hosts and which new access they enable
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for harvest additional credentials from compromised hosts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify deprecated SSL/TLS protocol versions
items.description: SSLv2/SSLv3 and TLS 1.0/1.1 are deprecated and vulnerable (POODLE, BEAST); only TLS 1.2+ should be enabled. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — TLS Configuration: Verify which protocol versions are accepted and flag any service still negotiating SSLv3/TLS 1.0/1.1
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify deprecated ssl/tls protocol versions, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet

items.title: Detect weak cipher suites and key exchange
items.description: NULL, EXPORT, RC4, DES/3DES, and anonymous suites, or weak DH parameters, undermine confidentiality and integrity. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — TLS Configuration: Verify the negotiated cipher suites and flag weak/anonymous ciphers and small DH/RSA key sizes
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect weak cipher suites and key exchange, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet

items.title: Validate certificate trust, validity, and naming
items.description: Expired, self-signed, wrong-CN/SAN, or weak-signature certificates enable MITM and erode trust in the service. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — TLS Configuration: Verify certificate chain, expiry, hostname match, and signature algorithm; flag self-signed or expired certs on production
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for validate certificate trust, validity, and naming, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP Transport Layer Security Cheat Sheet

items.title: Test for known TLS vulnerabilities (Heartbleed, ROBOT)
items.description: Library-level flaws like Heartbleed (CVE-2014-0160) and ROBOT can leak memory or enable decryption regardless of config. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — TLS Configuration: Verify the service is not vulnerable to Heartbleed/ROBOT/CCS-injection and similar protocol/library issues
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for known tls vulnerabilities (heartbleed, robot), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test ARP spoofing on local segments
items.description: ARP spoofing places the tester on-path, enabling interception/modification of traffic between hosts on the same segment. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — MITM & Poisoning: Verify whether dynamic ARP inspection / port security is absent and what traffic becomes interceptable on-path
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test arp spoofing on local segments, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoning
items.description: When LLMNR/NBT-NS are enabled, spoofed responses coerce hosts to send NetNTLM hashes that can be cracked or relayed. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — MITM & Poisoning: Verify whether name-resolution poisoning yields NetNTLM hashes and which accounts respond
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test llmnr/nbt-ns/mdns poisoning, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test NTLM relay to unsigned hosts
items.description: Coerced NetNTLM authentication can be relayed to SMB/LDAP/HTTP targets lacking signing, yielding code execution or privilege escalation. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — MITM & Poisoning: Verify viable relay targets (signing not required) and demonstrate the access gained via relay safely
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test ntlm relay to unsigned hosts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Test for rogue DHCP and gateway redirection
items.description: A rogue DHCP server can set the tester as default gateway/DNS, redirecting traffic for interception. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — MITM & Poisoning: Verify whether DHCP snooping is absent and whether clients accept rogue gateway/DNS configuration
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for test for rogue dhcp and gateway redirection, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Sniff for cleartext credentials on the wire
items.description: Protocols like HTTP, FTP, Telnet, POP3, IMAP, and SNMP v1/v2c carry credentials in cleartext that can be captured on-path. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — Cleartext Protocols & Sniffing: Verify captured credentials/sessions from a controlled position and document which protocols exposed them
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for sniff for cleartext credentials on the wire, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Inventory cleartext protocols in use
items.description: Identifying all unencrypted protocols across the estate quantifies sniffing exposure and guides remediation toward encrypted equivalents. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — Cleartext Protocols & Sniffing: Verify which cleartext services are still in use and whether encrypted alternatives are enforced
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for inventory cleartext protocols in use, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect sensitive data transmitted in cleartext
items.description: Beyond credentials, cleartext traffic may expose PII, tokens, internal hostnames, and business data subject to interception. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — Cleartext Protocols & Sniffing: Verify what sensitive data types appear in captured traffic and assess regulatory/business impact
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect sensitive data transmitted in cleartext, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Assess broadcast/multicast and VLAN exposure
items.description: Broadcast/multicast traffic and weak VLAN segmentation can expose data and name-resolution requests to attackers on the segment. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Transport & Sniffing — Cleartext Protocols & Sniffing: Verify what broadcast/multicast traffic is observable and whether VLAN hopping or weak segmentation increases sniffing scope
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for assess broadcast/multicast and vlan exposure, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.
